session_log.summary: Penetrated mysteries of the Bramblewood, encountered skeletons commandeered by mysterious cloaked figure, escaped with loot

locations: Lean-to west of the Bramblewood (NW of Tagmira)
session_log.summary: The party traveled to the lean-to NW of Tagmira, previously used as a base during early Bramblewood exploration, and found a troll had taken up residence. They killed it in a brutal fight that nearly cost Sardet his life, and recovered a valuable crown and jewels. Returning to Tagmira, they learned the Bronze Host has declared the hunting grounds NW of the town off-limits, and a local hunter is missing. Pima, a young girl who uses a mirror to communicate with fairie, implored the party to defeat the Bronze Host. Tagmira is gripped by fear.

combat.notes: Troll had taken up residence in the lean-to; nearly killed Sardet

social.description: Pima asks the party to defeat the Bronze Host
social.notes: Pima is a girl with a mirror that enables communication with fairie

exploration.description: Lean-to west of the Bramblewood, NW of Tagmira
exploration.notes: Previously used as a party base camp during early Bramblewood exploration

npcs_met: Pima (young girl with a mirror enabling communication with fairie)
rumors_learned: The Bronze Host has declared the hunting grounds NW of Tagmira off-limits
A local hunter has gone missing in the affected area
quests_started: Investigate the Bronze Host's ban on the hunting grounds NW of Tagmira and find the missing hunter
significant_events: Troll killed at the lean-to; Sardet nearly slain
Bronze Host declares hunting grounds NW of Tagmira off-limits
Pima requests the party defeat the Bronze Host

locations: Tazuln's shelter, north of Orziben (Chekkad's training)
Unplundered tomb south of Dravesa (Zardoon's training — adventure site stub)
session_log.summary: The party returned from the Bronze Host tower to Tagmira without incident, arriving on Keltoi 12. The following day was the feast of Lugazil — the Lugus festival of craft, speech, and binding laws. Ossivan Carn, a traveling lay-judge on his Marches circuit, was present and selected Strummer as his flyting opponent in the evening entertainment. The session covered a long span of in-game time as the party split up for training. Strummer advanced to 4th level as a bard, his Lugazil flyting against Ossivan Carn marking the culmination of that arc. Sardet rode south to Veltharoch for his Ardanir examination, advanced to 5th level, received the commission known as the Debt of Living Souls — a lifelong charge to bring the undead back into the divine order — and was given a grey warhorse provisionally as Veltharos's gift. Chekkad traveled north of Orziben to find Tazuln, an illusionist hermit, and advanced to 4th level; Tazuln reframed the entire basis of illusionist magic — illusions are not fabrications but resonance, light and form bleeding through from adjacent worlds in a twelve-world cosmology linked by twenty portals. Zardoon fell in with Ossek Thrice-Buried, a lore-obsessed antiquarian, who led him to an unplundered tomb south of Dravesa in a jackalwere-infested area; Zardoon advanced to 5th level through this excursion, which stands as an adventure site for future development. The party reconvened at Nemetra by Ragaia 11.

social.description: Lugazil flyting — Ossivan Carn vs. Strummer (Tagmira, Keltoi 13)
social.notes: Ossivan Carn selected Strummer as flyting opponent in the evening entertainment. The exchange happened. Noted as minor; see strummer_training.md for full scene prep.

social.description: Illusionist training — Tazuln north of Orziben (Chekkad)
social.notes: Tazuln's shelter is hard to find — eleven holes in a hillside ceiling, no path. Milky eyes, constant movement, hands scarred, clumsy and yet never loses anything, always knows who is in the room. Tazuln opened by telling Chekkad exactly which world his illusion was real in. Then laid out the cosmological structure: 12 worlds, 20 portals at the vertices of a truncated dodecahedron. Illusions are resonance — light and form bleeding through from adjacent worlds, or generated by their magical overlap. Not trickery. Physics. Chekkad cast something and for the first time knew where it was coming from.

social.description: Thief training — Ossek Thrice-Buried, tomb south of Dravesa (Zardoon)
social.notes: Ossek Thrice-Buried — small, crooked, bad knees, overfull satchel, smells of lamp oil. Walks past Ilmenor inquisitors on improvised credentials alone. Led Zardoon to an unplundered tomb south of Dravesa in jackalwere country. Ossek needs Zardoon's hands, eyes, and feet; offers tomb layout, inscription reading, and Aurka contacts. Mentions his dead partners Nevret, Bressul, and Davanek with unsettling frequency. Tomb is an adventure site stub — not fully cleared or developed.

social.description: Ardanir examination — unnamed examiner at Veltharoch
social.notes: Older man, scar along the jaw. Sparse hall, Veltharos shrine (lightning bolt, no crown — open sky). Sardet was confronted directly about the sphere and its blood cost. Examiner delivered the Debt of Living Souls: bring the undead back into divine order, let them die true deaths, see the souls owed to the gods repaid. No timeline given — it may be a life's work. Iron token of commission left on the bench between them. A grey warhorse was given provisionally — Veltharos's gift, not yet Sardet's.

exploration.notes: Travel montage. No combat, no notable incidents.

npcs_met: Ossivan Carn (lay-judge of Lugus, Marches circuit, originally Velgroth)
Tazuln 'the Seer' (illusionist hermit, north of Orziben — Chekkad's trainer)
Ossek Thrice-Buried (antiquarian, tomb-diver — Zardoon's contact; wants Ameniseb's tomb inscriptions)
Unnamed Ardanir examiner at Veltharoch (scar along jaw; represents Veltharos's order)
items_gained: Iron token of commission, storm-marked (Sardet — Debt of Living Souls)
Grey warhorse, provisional (Sardet — Veltharos's gift, not yet fully his)
quests_started: The Debt of Living Souls (Sardet) — a standing commission from the Ardanir order to bring the undead back into divine order: convert them, grant them true deaths, repay the souls owed to the gods. No deadline. May be his life's work.
significant_events: Party returned from Bronze Host tower to Tagmira — that phase of the arc concluded
Lugazil observed in Tagmira — Ossivan Carn present; flyting with Strummer occurred
Chekkad advanced to illusionist level 4 — trained by Tazuln; illusionist magic reframed as resonance from adjacent worlds in a twelve-world cosmology
Sardet advanced to paladin level 5 — Debt of Living Souls commission received; grey warhorse given provisionally
Ossivan Carn established as a recurring figure — writes everything down, circuit judge, neutral and formidable
Tazuln established as Chekkad's ongoing mentor — seeks a student who won't flinch at what the other worlds look like
Zardoon advanced to thief level 5 — partnered with Ossek Thrice-Buried; tomb south of Dravesa established as an adventure site stub
Ossek Thrice-Buried introduced — recurring antiquarian contact for Zardoon, Aurka network access, jackalwere country expertise
Party reconvened at Nemetra by Ragaia 11
session_log.dm_notes: Sardet now carries the Debt of Living Souls as a permanent background quest — every undead encounter has a hook attached. The grey warhorse is a dangling thread: Veltharos gave it provisionally, which means it can be taken back or fully granted depending on Sardet's conduct. Ossivan Carn is available to be hired, cited, or pointed at enemies on future circuits — he keeps notes on everyone and cannot be controlled once engaged. His presence at Lugazil means he now has a record of the party in Tagmira. Tazuln is a long-term mentor for Chekkad and a potential portal-hunting thread — he wants to reach one of the twenty vertices before he dies; Chekkad's training opens the door to that quest. The twelve-world cosmology with truncated dodecahedron portal geometry is now canon; Tazuln's shelter north of Orziben should get a settlement or location record. Ossek Thrice-Buried is Zardoon's recurring contact — can be pointed at tombs, used for Aurka introductions, or hired for inscription work; he cannot be controlled and will get people killed pursuing a rubbing. The unplundered tomb south of Dravesa is an adventure site stub: jackalwere-infested area, tomb interior unknown, Ossek's interest is inscriptions (possibly connected to Ameniseb and pre-Silence conditional immortality). Needs a proper site file before the party returns.

locations: Decaying dock on the southwest shore of the lake south of Caelrun
Swamp path between Caelrun and Nebruz
session_log.summary: Party split their early time between Nemetra and Caelrun gathering intel on the gnoll threat to Nemetra. Mother Yaleth was the primary source on the Ember-Tongue and pack structure; Tikka was interrogated and confirmed details, including Skrix's tree-markings in the northern forest. The party considered a Skrix extraction but did not act on it. They pivoted instead to the underwater investigation of the lake north of Caelrun, which required water-breathing potions. Yaleth supplied upland herbs for the brew (and tobacco for trade) and pointed them at Leeandra. Yavra ferried them to a decaying dock on the lake's southwest shore; from there the party took the swamp path to Nebruz, detecting and avoiding will-o-wisps en route. After overnighting in Nebruz they continued southwest into the swamp to Leeandra. Leeandra accepted the herbs and agreed to brew the potions over four days, but tasked the party with retrieving the brain fluid of a giant constrictor snake for her ring-lore. Session ended with the party preparing to depart on the constrictor hunt while the brew set.

social.description: Mother Yaleth at the herb-wives' compound (Nemetra)
social.notes: Extended interrogation about the Ember-Tongue, pack structure, and the Corrupted Grove. Yaleth supplies upland herbs for Leeandra's water-breathing brew and a parcel of tobacco — Yaleth is convinced tobacco will be profitable; Caelrun militia have already taken to it.
Standard gnoll briefing; militia cannot field a deep scout. No commitments asked or given.
Goblin refugee, male. Confirms Skrix is alive and carving spiral-slash warnings into trees in the northern forest. Adds detail to the gnoll picture but does not break new ground.
Ferried the party to the decaying dock on the southwest shore of the lake south of Caelrun, putting them on the swamp path to Nebruz.

Lizard-man diplomat and ring-lore practitioner. Accepted the herbs, agreed to brew water-breathing potions over four days, and dispatched the party to retrieve the brain fluid of a giant constrictor snake for her work. Established as the keystone NPC holding the Luric marches together.

exploration.description: Caelrun → southwest-shore dock → swamp path → Nebruz
exploration.notes: Will-o-wisps detected on the swamp path; party avoided them without engaging.

exploration.description: Overnight in Nebruz, then southwest through swamp to Leeandra's hut
exploration.notes: Leeandra's home is some hours southwest of Nebruz through the swamp.

npcs_met: Leeandra (lizard-man diplomat, swamp southwest of Nebruz)

Parcel of tobacco from Mother Yaleth (commercial trial)
rumors_learned: Skrix the goblin is alive and is carving spiral-slash warnings into trees in the northern forest
Tobacco is gaining traction with the Caelrun militia — Yaleth sees a trade opportunity
Leeandra is the diplomatic keystone keeping the lizard-man tribes from each other's throats and from Caelrun; the Luric marches depend on her

Underwater investigation of the lake north of Caelrun (latent, awaiting potions)
quests_pending_or_deferred: Disrupt or destroy the Gnoll Packs of the Ember-Tongue before they can move on Nemetra
significant_events: Party established Mother Yaleth as primary gnoll-country source and Leeandra as primary lizard-man contact
Pivot from gnoll-scout to lake-dive expedition; gnoll arc deferred
First overland traverse of the Caelrun → Nebruz swamp route; will-o-wisp threat noted but avoided
Tobacco entered Caelrun militia culture — first commercial breadcrumb for a future trade angle
Leeandra agreed to brew water-breathing potions (4-day timer) and assigned the constrictor brain-fluid quest
session_log.location_at_session_end: Leeandra's hut, swamp southwest of Nebruz
session_log.dm_notes: Most of the prepped gnoll-scout material (Nighthowl patrol encounter, Corrupted Grove vantage, sun-metal fragment, Kethraz's Ridge beat) rolls forward — bank for a later session when the party returns to the gnoll thread. Skrix extraction is hanging unresolved; party is interested but has not committed. Leeandra now load-bearing for two arcs: water-breathing potions (4-day brew clock) and the constrictor brain-fluid hunt (session 28 driver). Tobacco trial planted as a slow-burn commercial subplot. Yavra surfaced as a Caelrun NPC — needs a fuller record. Nebruz overnight not detailed yet; settlement notes pending.
